text:
Hey. So this week has been a lot, huh. The kind where you're not even sure which thing knocked you flat first, just that you're down here now looking up. I want you to know that's allowed. You don't have to spin it into a lesson or find the silver lining yet. Sometimes a week is just bad, and saying so out loud is its own small relief.

Here's the thing I keep coming back to: a terrible week is a unit of time, not a verdict on you. It feels permanent because you're inside it, the way weather feels like the whole sky when you're standing in the rain. But you've made it through every single one of your worst days so far, which is a flawless record if you think about it. You're already carrying more than most people would guess from looking at you, and you're still here, still trying, still reaching for something a little better. That's not nothing. That's actually most of what courage is.

So tonight, lower the bar on purpose. Drink some water, eat something that isn't sad, put on the show you've seen a hundred times, and let tomorrow be a smaller, more ordinary day. You don't have to fix everything. You just have to get to the other side of this week, and it's already almost over. I'm glad you're here. Genuinely. Go be gentle with yourself for a bit. You've earned it.
